reviews.title: False Advertising
reviews.negatives:
Booked through booking. Com. For 3 people. Could only book for 2 on the site. Emailed hotel with no response for months until we were in Aruba. We have been to Aruba several times and wanted to see Flamingo island. We were told that the 2 queen bed room was only for 2 people. Why have 2 beds and only allow for 2 people. In order to add our third person we had to upgrade to the ocean suite for an additional $250. This "upgrade" provided us with a bed and a sofa!!?? How is this an upgrade? We were overlooking the parking lot and had to shuttle to the main building for the dining options. Had to call maintenance for an air filter due to the loud whistle at 11 pm. Got no sleep. Our upgraded room only came with enough coffee and towels for 2. Outside smelled like the septic system was underfoot.

The island itself was unbelievably nothing like it is pictured!!! There are 8 flamingos in total! No wide stretch of beach. Not enough umbrellas for all the people. Drinks and food overpriced.

What is represented is definitely not what is provided. We were so unhappy we left after the first day $900 dollars wasted. So happy to be back at our room at the Divi Phoenix.

reviews.title: Massive billing mistakes, no apology or responsability
reviews.text: We stayed only 3 nights and we were wrongly charged twice the amount (aprox. $3200 US dollars) at the check out. I only noticed my card was maxed out completely when I received a notification from the bank that I had missed a mortgage payment, was going to incur overdraft charges and other bills were not going through - which was an extremely stressful way to end our holiday. I have contacted the management/ sales department with screenshots of my bank account, billing and a written statement from my bank regarding these charges and their response was disgraceful! They didn’t take responsibility for the mistake, they showed no intentions of sorting it out and refund and never even once apologised or offer compensation for the colossal incompetence. This is not what I expected from a Marriot Hotel and to be honest I wouldn’t trust them again. The only way I could retrieve the surplus $1400 US dollars wrongfully charged was by raising a fraud claim with my bank.

reviews.positives: The view from the room was really nice and a great first impression with the marina right there! We had extremely high hopes as we arrived during a Carnival parade. The steakhouse and buffet were great with the all inclusive package, allowed us to always sit down and get a great meal.
reviews.negatives:
The value for this resort and all inclusive package is not worth it. Point blank. The photos on Booking.com sell a grand island oasis, but it fell short to our expectations. The flamingo island was a massive let down, we couldn't get past the man made feel of the lagoons and longed for the open ocean. The drinks - we took two months off of drinking prior to the trip and we could NOT get even a buzz on the drink package. Shot after shot, drink after drink - nothing. We went out on the town one night, immediately drunk. Do with that info what you will. 

Overall, I would not stay here again. I would recommend anyone coming to Aruba to save your money, stay in a condo or AirBnB, maybe check out The Amsterdam. Rent a car. Spend your money at local restaurants. Go to the beaches that are not the flamingo island.

reviews.title: Bon établissement
reviews.text: Nous avons passé deux nuits dans cet établissement. Points positifs : chambre confortable, belles piscines, belle plage privée avec assez de places confortables pour passer un beau moment de détente, bon petit-déjeuner (mais cher). Points négatifs : boissons du bar chères, menu restaurant cher. L'île des flamants roses, propriété de l'hôtel,  a été une déception: deux belles plages avec une dizaine de flamants circulant de l'une à l'autre. Il vaut mieux y aller tôt le matin pour en profiter avant l'arrivée du trop grand nombre de touristes (clients de l'hôtel). Les flamants (flamingos) sont magnifiques, mais ils ne sont pas sauvages comme le prétend l'hôtel.  Apprivoisés, ils ont les ailes coupées et ne volent donc pas, incapables de quitter la petite île. Ils sont donc constamment suivis et photographiés par les touristes du matin au soir.

reviews.title: Not value for money
reviews.text:
Vi boede på dette hotel i 8 nætter.
PRisen tage i betragtning var dette hotel ikke pengene værd. De har 2 hoteller, et som ligger ved stranden og et der ligger ved havnen. Dette er desværre ikke særligt godt forklaret på agoda og derfor troede vi at vi have booket det ved stranden, men endte med at bo ved havnen. Vi har aldrig boet på et hotel hvor vi måtte vente ved morgenmaden fordi de simpelt ikke havde faciliteter nok til at håndtere alle de gæster de have boende. Morgenmaden var meget amerikansk ligesom resten at området er alt er tilrettet de mange amerikanske turister, desværre. Standarden på værelserne var skuffende og hotellet virker noget brugt og slidt. Lydisoleringen på værelserne var heller ikke så godt og der var en del larm fra gangen, også når hotelpersonalet talte i deres radioer. Det mest positive ved dette ophold var at hotellet også har en privat ø som man kan komme gratis til hver 15 min.

reviews.title: Could be better...
reviews.positives: Overall it's OK, but not above it in basically everything. OK beach, but small and unimpressive, OK pool, OK (but overpriced) food, OK drinks. One good thing - enough beach chairs and umbrellas, never had problems finding one.
reviews.negatives: The room is good, but the bathroom has no hooks to hang towels. Zero. Reading light from time to time spontaneously go out. VERY IMPORTANT: the hotel consists of two properties, one is beachfront (and the parking lot is there), and the other is 10 min walk. They never explain that on the web page, so you are under impression you would stay at a beachfront hotel, which is not the case. Some people said we should have handed a $50 bill at the checkin, and then they would give us a room in the beachfront building. Don't know. The private beach is a small lagoon cordoned off the see by a rock barrier. There are some fish, but they don't give you snorkels (contrary to the description), you can only get then at the island. The promised kayaks you cannot get anywhere, not even on the island.

reviews.positives: We stayed in an ocean-view room for four nights. The ocean view was very good in the morning and evening. Good location. Many restaurants were within walking distance. We did not hear the noise from upstairs, downstairs, or next door. The staff were very friendly and helpful. The best part and the reason we chose to stay in Renaissance Wind Creek was free access to Flaming Island. That was worth the money.

reviews.title: Great Private Island
reviews.positives:
When you book here, you get access to the private island where the flamingos live. Tickets there for non guests are around $135? So if you utilize going to this beautiful island, you get your money's worth from this hotel. I highly recommend doing it! 

The hotel had a lot of shopping, dining, casino, and access to 3 pools. We stayed at the adults only section of the hotel, and the pool was nice but much smaller than the one at the family-friendly one. The family-friendly one had a swim up bar, so be sure to go there as well.

The hotels are in the heart of Orjanstead, so you can easily walk to other restaurants and bars.
reviews.negatives: The hotel is expensive, in my opinion, if you don't utilize the island option. The room is small (but modern), and the breakfast included was very mediocre. I've stayed at other Renaissance Hotels worldwide, and they've done better with the rooms and breakfasts. We had some great experiences with some staff half the time, while the other half of the time, the staff seemed stressed/too busy.

reviews.title: Relaxing
reviews.positives: Distance from airport, comfortable rooms, water taxi to Flamingo Beach, shops and boutiques and casino all on premises
reviews.negatives: The size of the pool. In my opinion, it could stand to be a bit larger especially with the amount of guests that stay there the pool area could be larger.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives:
The people could not have been nicer. It was truly just such a great experience, and the hotel staff were incredibly friendly. I truly felt like everyone was really happy to help, however possible, and were authentically just so happy to welcome us in for our vacation and show us a good time. Twice, we went to hand a cash tip as a 'thank you', and had the people refuse to take it, telling us "it's a pleasure and blessing to have you here". We're truly not any kind of special guests, but were certainly made to feel that way many times. 

The location, and not being on the Eagle Beach strip, was actually a major plus. We had to take taxis for all excursions and dinners out, which is an expense we were more than okay with. The main strip up near Eagle Beach is really touristy, and being removed from all of that by a 10-15 min taxi ride (~$15) was ideal. Worth noting, this hotel is walking distance from our favorite restaurant on the island, Lima. 

The beach was incredible. Our primary goal of this trip was maximizing beach time and just relaxing, and Renaissance Island was the perfect thing for that. After seeing the main beaches up north, we were so happy with our decision to stay here and have access to this private island. Get out there early for the best seats (more on that below).
reviews.negatives: The only thing I have a complaint about is that I think the pools and beaches could use a little more enforcement around chair saving without using them, or maybe a better system for reserving chairs. You have to be among the first on the island to get a good spot, which is fine, but some people go out there, claim a ton of chairs to themselves, and then go MIA for literally hours. We saw it every day that we were there. On one of the days, a group of 6 chairs sat "occupied" with towels on them (nobody actually using them) for over 5 hours. We heard that there's a general rule that if things are left unattended for over an hour, staff will clear the space and allow others to claim a chair, but that's not actually happening.

reviews.title: Renaissance Wind Creek: an ideal stay for couples seeking luxury and proximity to Aruba's finest.
reviews.positives: The Renaissance Marina Hotel in Oranjestad was an ideal place for our 1-week stay in Aruba as a couple. We greatly appreciated the separation of the family hotel (Ocean Suites) from the adults-only hotel (Marina Hotel) but enjoyed the fact that both facilities were accessible to us as guests of the Renaissance. The staff were wonderful and always helpful and knowledgeable when we had questions or requests. The inclusion of access to the Renaissance private island was a highlight of our stay, and we again appreciated the ability to enjoy an adults-only experience on that island. The location of the Marina Hotel in Oranjestad was also ideal for our ability to walk to dozens of restaurants, bars, and shops in the city within minutes. We felt safe, secure, and always had an easy time navigating this neighborhood of Oranjestad at all hours of the day and night.
reviews.negatives: While we were happy with the size and amenities of our hotel room (and the wonderful housekeeping staff), we found that the AC and bathroom fan weren't sufficient in drying out the room, which led to dampness and difficulty air-drying our clothes during our stay. The bedding, while clean and comfortable and changed regularly, developed a mildew-ish smell after a day or two that eventually became a constant, mild smell throughout the room. This was certainly not unfortunate enough to ruin our stay, but if we experience something similar in a future stay, we would be inclined to ask hotel staff for assistance with a deeper room clean or perhaps a move to a different room.

reviews.title: Flamingo, fun, and fiancé!
reviews.positives: Location is close to the airport and pretty easy to get a cab to other areas. Pool is nice and having the boat take you to the beach is clutch. The shuttle to the other for the gym is also helpful. The staff also host fun events in the lobby which is nice! They were also super helpful to my now fiancé in planning his proposal. Arlyn you are a superstar! Kym helped us find yummy foods off the beaten path and hooked me up with the turle watchers info. Cris at reception was always and helpful when needed. Jorge always greeted us warmly upon returning from our excursion and also gave some good recs. The gentleman driving the boat shuttles were super chill and nice to chat with during 10-15 minutes rides. They were super informative about the route they took and if they expected the water to be choppy. Just all around the staff was awesome. I’m very happy we went with Renaissance.
reviews.negatives: Nothing too big but it looks like the curtains need replacing. They have tiny holes probably from moths.

reviews.title: I loved it!
reviews.positives:
It is beautiful,  welcoming, relaxing and the staff are amazing.  From the bellhops to the front desk, hosuekeeping, and the restaurants, everyone is friendly, considerate and caring.
The room was very nice and the bed was comfortable.   I treated myself to a spa and Ms. Gloria was my masseuse.  She was excellent!!!!!! The food was nice, although I found it to be a little pricey.  The private beach, and the boat ride to the private beach, are worth the money.  This is my second time staying at the Renaissance Auba and I will definitely go back again.
reviews.negatives: I thought the breakfast was overpriced at 35.00.  At best, they should have charged 15.00, and that's being generous.

reviews.title: Superb Location with Great Hospitality!
reviews.positives:
We like the location being at the center of the city and with so many places to eat and explore.  Great vibe all around!  The private island is definitely an awesome perk! We chose this place for our honeymoon then and came back to celebrate our 25th wedding anniversary - we had an AWESOME time! 

If our adult children will get married in the near future, we are happy to send them to Renaissance Wind Creek Resort for their honeymoon ~ IF they want to :)

reviews.title: SUPERB!!
reviews.positives: Everything!  We stayed at the family side of the property, and it was an incredible experience.  The property of the resort is spectacular with sea side walkways with breathtaking ocean views, gorgeous pools, hot tubs and cafes. Birdsong awaits you as you relax fine or stroll amongst the palms and tropical flowers within the gardens of this resort.  The boat taxi to Flamingo Island was above and beyond our expectations.  The snorkeling was amazing!! The food is delicious and fairly priced.  The rooms are very clean and updated.  The staff are so pleasant and helpful.  We loved Aruba and look forward to returning again soon, and will definitely book again with Renaissance Wind Creek.

reviews.title: A fabulous Caribbean vacation filled with great amenities for a very reasonable price!
reviews.positives: The adult-only section of the Renaissance Wind Creek resort was perfect for a romantic couples' vacation. Some of the finest restaurants in Aruba are a short (10-15 mins), safe walk from the marina resort. The private island provided a quiet space to enjoy the beaches, and the flamingoes were an extra special attraction on the island. The boat ride to the island was quick and easy to access. All the staff in every position along the way were extremely helpful and friendly! And having the casino down the hall was an added bonus for a fun-filled, adult vacation!
reviews.negatives: The bedding was comfortable, but the pillows were way too soft and flimsy. It seems they need to add some new pillows into the rooms. A point for improvement on the island would be a guide/host to explain all the wildlife on the beaches and in the waterways.

reviews.title: Perfect Relaxing Stay
reviews.positives: The property is beautiful and I loved that it was opened space to the outside. We stayed on the marina side and had a relaxing stay. The staff were friendly and not over baring. I love that it’s across the street from other bars, restaurants, and stores. It was also very clean. The room was big enough for me and my husband and having the small balcony allowed us to take in some fresh air and the music from the beach bar. Also having access to flamingo island was such a blessing. The boat ride was quick and easy to access. The beach was comfortable and had plenty of chairs for us to sit and the bar was an extra plus! We enjoyed our stay and will be back with the kiddos.

Ms Deidra also checked on us every time she saw us to be sure we were getting everything we needed.  The breakfast at Aquarius was great especially the made to order omelets!
